statement: Restatement: Let G be a finite group with |G| = p where p is prime. Then there exists g ∈ G such that ⟨g⟩ = G.
By Lagrange's theorem, the order of any element divides |G| = p, so every non-identity element has order p.
A group of prime order has no nontrivial proper subgroups.
If p ≥ 2, then p > 1 ensures existence of a non-identity element.
